commit.message:
CFL-082: CTiffImg::ReadLine() strip buffer bounds check

Fixes heap-buffer-overflow in CTiffImg::ReadLine() at TiffImg.cpp:378.
When StripByteCounts is smaller than m_nRowsPerStrip * m_nBytesPerLine,
ReadLine() reads past the end of the strip buffer via memcpy.

Root cause: Open() allocates m_pStripBuf sized by TIFFStripSize() but
ReadLine() accesses nRowOffset * m_nBytesPerLine without bounds check.

Fix: After malloc in Open(), validate that the allocated buffer is large
enough for the declared row geometry. Returns false for malformed TIFFs.

CWE-122 (Heap-based Buffer Overflow) / CWE-125 (Out-of-bounds Read)
Crash: crash-3c9fd44b5e25285f5fc22b0941447dd86f55d9c5
Affects: iccApplyProfiles, iccSpecSepToTiff, any tool using CTiffImg

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: always restore window layout on startup regardless of mode

startup() now calls restore_session() first (layout + buffers),
then applies the startup mode (journal opens today's journal,
restore keeps session state, blank opens scratch). Previously
journal mode skipped restore entirely, losing window splits.

The startup mode controls which buffer gets focus, not whether
the layout is preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 3 Step 7: Implement Xe batch submission

Extends render-sys/src/xe.rs with high-level batch submission methods
parallel to the existing i915 implementation.

New functionality:
- xe_submit_batch(): Full submission with VM/exec queue creation and
  synchronous wait
- xe_submit_batch_simple(): Simplified one-shot submission interface
- xe_create_context(): Create reusable VM and exec queue pair

New types:
- EngineInstance: Select render/compute/copy engines for exec queues
- SyncObject: Fence-based synchronization (for future async support)

Implementation details:
- Follows same pattern as i915_submit_batch for API consistency
- Handles Xe's explicit VM model (create VM, bind buffers, submit)
- Defaults to render engine (gt_id=0) for graphics workloads
- Graceful fallback when Xe driver unavailable

Testing:
- All 100 Rust tests pass (4 new Xe submission tests)
- All Go tests pass (17 packages)
- Static linking verified
- Zero regressions in complexity or duplication
- Test coverage: 100% for public API

Next step: Step 8 (Go CGO bindings for submission)

commit.message:
feat(game): audio-only matchups and listen-weighted ELO voting

Matchup:
- Filter rankings pool to countries with audio_url only — no more
  silent matchups where neither anthem can be played

Vote — remove listen gate, add weighted ELO:
- Drop the MIN_LISTEN_MS hard gate (was 3 s); votes are now always
  accepted regardless of listen time
- ELO change is scaled by vote_weight = listenWeight(winner) × listenWeight(loser)
  where listenWeight = clamp(totalListenMs / 10_000, 0, 1)
- Both anthems need >= 10 s of cumulative listening (any round) for
  a full-weight (1.0) opinion; otherwise impact is proportional
- vote_weight is returned in the response so the UI can show it
- Listen history writes now store the computed cumulative total rather
  than adding an increment (avoids drift across parallel writes)

Frontend (game.js):
- Remove MIN_LISTEN_MS constant and alreadyHeardA/B state
- Vote buttons are always enabled when a matchup renders — no timer
  gate blocking the user
- Flash message shows vote weight hint when < 100% (e.g. '73% weight
  — listen longer for full impact')
- 422 handler removed (server no longer returns 422 for listen time)

Tests:
- Replace '422 when listen insufficient' test with:
  'accepts vote with minimal listen time (partial weight)' — asserts
  vote_weight in [0,1] and < 1 for short listens
  'accepts vote and returns vote_weight' — asserts vote_weight = 1
  for full 10 s listen
- Add 4 ELO unit tests covering: full weight, default args, fractional
  weight at 5 s each (0.25), and zero weight with no ELO movement

commit.message:
perf: optimize mask rendering pipeline (#151)

Reduce memory allocation and improve per-pixel luminance math in
the mask rendering path:

- In-place alpha modification: apply mask luminance directly to the
  source image instead of allocating a separate output buffer
- Reusable mask buffer: lazily allocated BufferedImage cached on
  Surface, cleared with Arrays.fill between calls
- Reusable effect buffer: shared off-screen buffer for
  filter/mask/opacity elements with nesting guard for recursive
  draw() calls
- Fast integer /255: replace long division by 16,646,400 with
  two-step (x+1+(x>>8))>>8 trick
- Early exits: skip zero-alpha source pixels, zero-alpha mask
  pixels, and zero-luminance mask pixels

commit.message:
feat: add postgres restore script with safe input validation (#21)

## Summary

Adds `scripts/restore.sh` — an interactive PostgreSQL restore utility.

### Key validation fix
Instead of using `sed -n "${CHOICE}p"` (which silently returns empty
string for out-of-range input), the script uses `mapfile` to load
backups into an array and validates the choice before indexing:

```bash
if ! [[ "$CHOICE" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || (( CHOICE < 1 || CHOICE > ${#BACKUPS[@]} )); then
    die "Invalid selection: $CHOICE (must be between 1 and ${#BACKUPS[@]})"
fi
```

This emits `Invalid selection: N` immediately rather than the confusing
`File not found: ` that would result from an empty `BACKUP_FILE`.

### Features
- Interactive mode: lists `.sql.gz` backups from
`$DEPLOY_DIR/data/backups` sorted newest-first, prompts for numeric
selection
- Non-interactive mode: `restore.sh <file>` for scripted use
- Requires typing `yes` before dropping and recreating the database
- Finds the running Postgres container by stack service name
automatically

---------

Fix Metal SIGABRT: struct alignment + zero-size guard

Three fixes for the Metal renderer crash on device:

1. AuraUniforms struct reordered for Metal alignment: moved pulse
   before resolution so float3 seedColor sits at offset 16 (natural
   alignment). Now size == stride == 32, matching MSL layout exactly.

2. Changed MemoryLayout<T>.size → .stride in both AuraRenderer and
   EmeraldRenderer to include any tail padding Metal expects.

3. Added drawableSize > 0 guard in drawFullscreenQuad to skip
   rendering before the view has been laid out (prevents divide-by-
   zero in the shader when resolution is (0, 0)).

Also removed multicast entitlement (requires Apple approval; Bonjour
NWBrowser/NWListener work without it on local networks).

commit.message:
fix: app_environment rename, mobile secrets, env template cleanup (#20)

## Summary

### fix: rename Ansible variable `environment` → `app_environment`
`environment` is a reserved Ansible keyword (used to set process env
vars at task level). Passing it via `-e environment=...` shadowed the
built-in, causing `{{ environment }}` in the Jinja2 template to render
as `[]` instead of the expected string value.

- `.github/workflows/setup-server.yml`: `-e "app_environment=${{
secrets.ENVIRONMENT }}"` (both jobs)
- `ansible/playbooks/templates/env.j2`: `ENVIRONMENT={{ app_environment
}}`

### feat: add `APP_METRICA_KEY` and `IU_ALUMNI_WEB_SALT` GitHub Actions
secrets for mobile
New Terraform resources in `environments.tf` + variables in
`variables.tf` to push these secrets into the `iu-alumni-mobile` testing
and production environments.

### fix: env template and workflow cleanup
- Removed duplicate `ENVIRONMENT` entry from `env.j2`
- Added `MAIL_FROM_NAME` that was missing
- Added Docker image defaults (`BACKEND_IMAGE`, `FRONTEND_IMAGE`,
`MOBILE_IMAGE`)
- Added `data/backups` deploy directory in `setup-server.yml`

feat: inline completion for [[ (link) and # (tag) triggers in Insert mode

Add InlineCompletion state to BloomEditor with Link and Tag variants.
Detect [[ and # triggers after edits in Insert mode, populate the
existing InlineMenuFrame with filtered page/tag items anchored at the
cursor, and intercept Enter/Tab/Esc/Up/Down to accept, cancel, or
navigate the completion menu.

- Add InlineCompletion struct and InlineCompletionKind enum (lib.rs)
- Trigger detection in check_inline_triggers() after VimAction::Edit
- Accept logic replaces trigger text with [[id|title]] or #tagname
- Cancel on Esc, mode change, or cursor motion
- Render inline menu with Cursor anchor when completion is active
- Add optional id field to InlineMenuItem for link page ids
